input.rfq_title: Request for Quotation (RFQ) - GlowBite 18-Month Go-to-Market Marketing Services Program

clarification_questions.question: Regarding your pricing for line item 2, your response does not include the price for this item. Could you provide the specific fee for line_items[2]?

vendor_readiness.descriptor: 1 of 6 dimensions comparable; blocked on technical, commercial, scope, timeline, compliance
3 of 6 dimensions comparable; blocked on commercial, timeline, compliance
5 of 6 dimensions comparable; blocked on commercial

verdicts.reason: This response covers several line items, but TVC Production scope is missing and Kids Advertising & Claims Compliance scope is only stated in general terms, so the technical offer is incomplete.
All bid line items have present scope coverage statements and the proposal addresses the full RFQ program at a usable technical level.
The proposal provides present scope coverage across all RFQ line items, even where some workstreams are bundled commercially.

dimensions.narrative: The technical comparison is uneven across vendors. polished-fluff and thorough-but-pricey both present full-program scope coverage across all listed line items, while cheap-but-incomplete leaves material gaps, particularly for TVC Production and a clearly defined kids compliance workstream. Buyers can compare the latter two more directly on technical breadth, but cheap-but-incomplete needs scope clarification before a like-for-like technical review is reliable.

verdicts.reason: Paid Media Buying pricing is unclear, Kids Advertising & Claims Compliance pricing is missing, TVC Production pricing is missing, and no total price is provided.
Commercial detail is broad and mostly present, but pricing structure and total price are internally conflicting and several figures are given as ranges rather than fixed amounts.
Several major line items are commercially bundled or unclear, TVC Production pricing is conflicting, and no total price is provided.

dimensions.narrative: Commercial comparability is limited across all three responses. cheap-but-incomplete and thorough-but-pricey both have blocking gaps because key line-item pricing is missing, unclear, or bundled in ways that prevent a clean line-by-line comparison, while polished-fluff is more complete but still uses ranges and contains conflicting total commercial figures. Further buyer review should focus first on resolving missing and inconsistent pricing before drawing commercial conclusions.

verdicts.reason: At least one RFQ item has missing scope coverage, with TVC Production absent and Kids Advertising & Claims Compliance not clearly mapped to the requested deliverables.
Each RFQ line item has present scope coverage, allowing item-by-item review across the full requested program.
All RFQ line items are addressed with present scope coverage, although some are packaged together commercially.

dimensions.narrative: On scope completeness, polished-fluff and thorough-but-pricey both address all RFQ line items, which supports a fuller side-by-side review. cheap-but-incomplete does not, because one line item is missing entirely and another is only loosely described relative to the RFQ's compliance-specific requirements. As a result, scope comparison is currently strongest between the two fuller submissions.

verdicts.reason: The response gives several item-specific timing references, but the overall schedule is not consolidated and some timing is conditional or vague.
Timeline information is present but internally conflicting, including inconsistent duration references for production and launch management.
A program timeline is present and grounded, even though it is stated at a higher level than a full workback schedule.

dimensions.narrative: Timeline comparability is mixed rather than clean. cheap-but-incomplete provides fragmented line-item timing without a clear integrated schedule, and polished-fluff contains conflicting duration statements that need reconciliation. thorough-but-pricey is the clearest at overall program level, but buyers would still need a more detailed milestone view to compare delivery readiness across all vendors.

verdicts.reason: The vendor provides some compliance claims and internal review language, but the statements are high level and do not clearly map to the RFQ's detailed child-advertising and claims-control requirements.
The proposal includes a compliance statement, but it is brief and less specific than the RFQ's requested frameworks, sign-off controls, and evidence handling expectations.
The proposal provides multiple specific compliance points, including written sign-off memos, disclosure language, and insurance confirmation, giving a fuller basis for compliance comparison.

dimensions.narrative: Compliance coverage varies significantly in depth. cheap-but-incomplete and polished-fluff both make positive compliance statements, but they remain relatively generic against the RFQ's detailed requirements for COPPA, claims substantiation, sign-off records, and governance controls. thorough-but-pricey gives the most explicit compliance content and is presently the strongest basis for compliance-side comparison.

verdicts.reason: The vendor identifies timeline and scope-change risks with specific examples, providing a usable risk view.
The proposal identifies delivery and scope-change risks and describes a recovery approach if milestones come under pressure.
The response contains multiple concrete risk statements covering timeline, budget flex, inventory constraints, and escalation triggers.

dimensions.narrative: Risk disclosure is the most comparable dimension across the three submissions. All vendors identify at least some delivery or scope-related risks, though the depth varies, with thorough-but-pricey providing the most detailed set of operational risks. This dimension can be reviewed comparatively now, while noting that the quality and specificity of mitigation detail is not uniform.

line_item_offers.scope_verbatim: We would run this as an 8-week sprint with a working session up front, stakeholder interviews as needed, audience review, messaging development and concept creation.
We would take the approved campaign platform into film territory and build out script and storyboard materials ready for production handoff.

Our scope here would cover:
- platform role definition for Instagram, TikTok and YouTube Shorts
- editorial calendar for launch period
- content bank of 36 finished pieces across static/carousel/short-form
- caption copy and CTA guidance
- template set for reactive content
- community management playbook
- influencer identification brief
- monthly performance summaries
- editable source files
We can support the 12-week launch planning across TV, digital and OOH with a channel strategy and investment framework.

In principle we can support:
- trafficking and booking coordination
- optimisation during campaign
- weekly and monthly reporting
- reconciliation support

Included:
- master plan/workback
- weekly status reporting
- weekly cross-agency coordination agenda and action tracking
- monthly leadership summary
- risk and change log
- launch readiness checklist
- post-launch retrospective

We will deliver a focused but high-value strategic foundation for GlowBite, bringing together stakeholder alignment, audience segmentation, positioning development, campaign platform creation, and launch messaging principles.
We will translate the approved platform into film-ready thinking, balancing emotional resonance, claim sensitivity, and efficient production planning.

Included agency/managed scope:

- Production management and oversight
- Pre-production coordination
- Casting supervision
- Location and shoot management
- Edit supervision
- Post-production oversight
- Master delivery management
- Digital cutdown adaptation stewardship

Scope includes:

- Platform role definition for Instagram, TikTok, and YouTube Shorts
- 12-week editorial planning
- 3-month content bank with launch-phase assets
- Captioning and CTA guidance
- Template system for reactive/community content
- Community management playbook
- Influencer identification brief
- Monthly performance summaries
- Editable source file handover

Planning outputs include:

- Integrated 12-week media plan across TV, digital video, paid social, and search where applicable
- Channel-mix rationale linked to audience priorities and launch objectives
- Reach and frequency projections with methodology notes
- TV schedule recommendation and flighting rationale
- Monthly pacing and investment phasing
- Parent/caregiver targeting framework and audience safeguards
- KPI and measurement framework
- One revised version following consolidated Luminos feedback
- Final deck and budget workbook
We will execute approved buying across TV and digital channels with full trafficking, optimisation, reporting, and reconciliation support.

Our role here would be to support confidence across script review, social asset review, claim sensitivity, and launch governance documentation, ensuring the programme is managed with care and responsibility throughout the campaign lifecycle.
We would provide a central PMO layer to coordinate workstreams, stakeholder decisions, dependencies, reporting, and launch readiness.
Kick-off workshop with Luminos stakeholders across brand, sales, regulatory, and procurement
Film adaptation briefing
Pre-production planning and scheduling
Platform-specific content strategy across Instagram, TikTok, and YouTube Shorts
Integrated 12-week media plan across TV, digital video, paid social, search where applicable, and OOH
Buying, booking, trafficking, optimisation, and reporting
Compliance framework document

line_item_offers.pricing_verbatim: 132,000 plus applicable statutory taxes and levies
line_item_offers.scope_verbatim: Master project plan in Gantt format
